name: Taj Mahal
description: Immense mausoleum of white marble built in Agra between 1631 and 1648 by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan.

name: Agra Fort
description: Massive 16th-century Mughal fort in Agra containing palaces, mosques and audience halls.

name: Ajanta Caves
description: Buddhist cave monuments dating from 2nd century BC with paintings and sculptures.

name: Ellora Caves
description: 34 rock-cut Buddhist, Hindu and Jain cave temples from 6th to 11th centuries.

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: 13th-century chariot-shaped temple dedicated to the Sun God on the Bay of Bengal coast.

name: Mahabodhi Temple Complex at Bodh Gaya
description: Site where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment under the Bodhi tree.

name: Qutb Minar and its Monuments
description: 73-metre minaret built in 1193 with surrounding ruins of the first mosque in India.

name: Hampi
description: Ruins of Vijayanagara Empire capital, including temples, palaces and market streets.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: Rock-cut cave temples dedicated to Shiva on an island in Mumbai harbour.

name: Rock Shelters of Bhimbetka
description: Prehistoric cave paintings spanning over 10,000 years in central India.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus
description: Victorian Gothic Revival-style railway station in Mumbai, built in 1887.

name: Red Fort Complex
description: Palatial fort in Delhi built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in 1639.

name: Jantar Mantar Jaipur
description: 18th-century astronomical observation site with massive masonry instruments.

name: Hill Forts of Rajasthan
description: Series of six majestic hill forts in the Aravalli hills of Rajasthan.

name: Rani-ki-Vav
description: Intricately constructed stepwell on the banks of the Saraswati River in Patan.

name: Nalanda Mahavihara
description: Archaeological site of Nalanda, a monastic and scholastic institution from 3rd century BC.

name: Khajuraho Group of Monuments
description: Group of Hindu and Jain temples known for their intricate erotic sculptures.

name: Buddhist Monuments at Sanchi
description: Buddhist sanctuaries dating back to the 3rd century BC including the Great Stupa.

name: Churches and Convents of Goa
description: Portuguese colonial churches including Basilica of Bom Jesus with relics of St. Francis Xavier.

name: Pattadakal Group of Monuments
description: 8th-century temples blending northern and southern Indian architectural styles.

name: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks
description: Remote Himalayan sanctuaries known for alpine flowers and snow leopards.

name: Kaziranga National Park
description: Park on the banks of the Brahmaputra with two-thirds of the world's great one-horned rhinoceroses.

name: Sundarbans National Park
description: Largest tidal mangrove forest in the world at the mouth of the Ganges.

name: Western Ghats
description: Mountain chain older than the Himalayas with exceptional biodiversity.

name: Angkor
description: Remains of the Khmer Empire capitals from 9th to 15th centuries, including Angkor Wat temple complex.

name: Angkor Wat
description: 12th-century temple complex and the largest religious monument in the world, built by the Khmer Empire.

name: Temple of Preah Vihear
description: 11th-century Hindu sanctuary on the edge of a cliff on the Dangrek Mountains.

name: Great Wall of China
description: Series of fortifications built across northern China over many centuries.

name: Forbidden City
description: Imperial palace complex in Beijing, home to 24 emperors of the Ming and Qing dynasties.

name: Mausoleum of the First Qin Emperor
description: Tomb complex of Emperor Qin Shi Huang including the famous Terracotta Army.

name: Mogao Caves
description: 492 cells and cave sanctuaries carved into cliffs along the ancient Silk Road.

name: Huangshan
description: Mountain range in eastern China renowned for scenery that has influenced Chinese art.

name: Jiuzhaigou Valley
description: Valley with colourful lakes and waterfalls in Sichuan Province.

name: Old Town of Lijiang
description: Ancient trading town of the Naxi people, well preserved for 800 years.

name: Summer Palace Beijing
description: Masterpiece of Chinese landscape garden design with Kunming Lake and Longevity Hill.

name: Temple of Heaven Beijing
description: Complex of Taoist buildings where emperors performed annual rituals for good harvests.

name: Longmen Grottoes
description: Buddhist cave temples carved from 493 AD containing over 100,000 Buddhist stone carvings.

name: Yungang Grottoes
description: Ancient Buddhist cave art from 5th and 6th centuries in Shanxi Province.

name: West Lake Cultural Landscape of Hangzhou
description: Cultural landscape that inspired garden designs in East Asia and the West.

name: Three Parallel Rivers of Yunnan Protected Areas
description: Gorges of the Yangtze, Mekong and Salween rivers in Yunnan Province.

name: Wulingyuan Scenic and Historic Interest Area
description: Over 3,000 quartzite sandstone pillars in Hunan Province.

name: Himeji Castle
description: Finest surviving example of early 17th-century Japanese castle architecture.

name: Buddhist Monuments in the Horyu-ji Area
description: Oldest surviving wooden structures in the world dating from 7th century.

name: Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto
description: 17 Buddhist temples, Shinto shrines and a castle from the ancient capital of Japan.

name: Historic Villages of Shirakawa-go and Gokayama
description: Farmhouses with steep thatched roofs in isolated mountain villages.

name: Hiroshima Peace Memorial
description: Genbaku Dome, the only structure left standing near the 1945 atomic bomb hypocenter.

name: Itsukushima Shinto Shrine
description: Shinto shrine on the island of Miyajima with famous floating torii gate.

name: Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara
description: Monuments representing the flowering of Japanese culture under Chinese influence.

name: Shrines and Temples of Nikko
description: Elaborate 17th-century shrines and mausoleums in a sacred mountain forest.

name: Fujisan Sacred Place
description: Sacred mountain and source of artistic inspiration for centuries of Japanese culture.

name: Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range
description: Sacred forests, shrines and pilgrimage routes in the mountainous Kii Peninsula.

name: Borobudur Temple Compounds
description: 9th-century Mahayana Buddhist temple, the world's largest Buddhist monument.

name: Prambanan Temple Compounds
description: 9th-century Hindu temple compound dedicated to the Trimurti in Java.

name: Komodo National Park
description: Islands of Komodo, Rinca and Padar with the last population of Komodo dragons.

name: Bali Cultural Landscape of Subak System
description: Landscape of water temples, rice paddies and rural villages in Bali.

name: Haeinsa Temple Janggyeong Panjeon
description: Depository for the Tripitaka Koreana woodblocks from the 13th century.

name: Seokguram Grotto and Bulguksa Temple
description: 8th-century Buddhist temple and artificial grotto with a granite Buddha.

name: Changdeokgung Palace Complex
description: Palace complex including the Secret Garden built during the Joseon Dynasty.

name: Gyeongju Historic Areas
description: Cultural landscape of the Silla kingdom with Buddhist temples and burial mounds.

name: Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes
description: Hallasan National Park, Geomunoreum lava tube system and Seongsan Ilchulbong tuff cone.

name: Ayutthaya Historic City
description: Capital of Ayutthaya Kingdom with Buddhist monuments from 1350 to 1767.

name: Sukhothai Historic Town
description: First capital of the Siamese Kingdom with monuments from the 13th century.

name: Hoi An Ancient Town
description: Exceptionally well-preserved trading port from the 15th to 19th centuries.

name: Hue Complex of Monuments
description: Imperial citadel and tombs of the Nguyen Dynasty emperors in central Vietnam.

name: My Son Sanctuary
description: Ruins of the Cham Empire Hindu temples from 4th to 13th centuries.

name: Ha Long Bay
description: Bay with 1,600 limestone islets forming a seascape of exceptional beauty.

name: Kathmandu Valley
description: Seven groups of Hindu and Buddhist monuments and buildings including Pashupatinath.

name: Sagarmatha National Park
description: National park containing Mount Everest with Sherpa cultural heritage.

name: Ancient City of Sigiriya
description: 5th-century rock fortress and palace with water gardens and frescoes.

name: Sacred City of Kandy
description: Last capital of the ancient Sinhala kingdom with the Temple of the Tooth.

name: Old Town of Galle and its Fortifications
description: Dutch colonial fortification founded in the 16th century on the southwest coast.

name: Bagan
description: Archaeological area with thousands of Buddhist temples from the 9th to 13th centuries.

name: Luang Prabang
description: Town at the junction of the Mekong and Nam Khan rivers with temples and colonial architecture.

name: George Town and Malacca
description: Historic cities on the Straits of Malacca with multi-cultural trading heritage.

name: Rice Terraces of the Philippine Cordilleras
description: 2,000-year-old terraces carved into the mountains by the Ifugao people.

name: Historic City of Vigan
description: Best preserved Spanish colonial town in Asia dating from the 16th century.

name: Acropolis Athens
description: Ancient citadel containing the Parthenon and other classical Greek monuments.

name: Delphi
description: Pan-Hellenic sanctuary and oracle of the god Apollo at the foot of Mount Parnassus.

name: Meteora
description: Monasteries perched atop towering natural sandstone pillars in Thessaly.

name: Mount Athos
description: Autonomous monastic state with 20 Eastern Orthodox Christian monasteries.

name: Historic Centre of Rome
description: Roman monuments including the Colosseum, Forum, Pantheon and Papal Rome.

name: Venice and its Lagoon
description: City built on 118 islands with canals and outstanding Renaissance architecture.

name: Historic Centre of Florence
description: Renaissance city with Brunelleschi's dome, Uffizi Gallery and Piazza della Signoria.

name: Piazza del Duomo Pisa
description: Square with the Cathedral, Baptistry, Campanile (Leaning Tower) and Monumental Cemetery.

name: Pompeii Herculaneum and Torre Annunziata
description: Roman towns preserved under volcanic ash from the 79 AD Vesuvius eruption.

name: Amalfi Coast
description: Outstanding example of a Mediterranean landscape with terraced vineyards and villages.

name: Cinque Terre
description: Five villages clinging to terraced hillsides on the Ligurian coast.

name: Aeolian Islands
description: Volcanic island archipelago north of Sicily with ongoing volcanic activity.

name: Historic Centre of Naples
description: One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world, founded by Greeks.

name: Stonehenge Avebury and Associated Sites
description: Prehistoric ceremonial and sacred sites including the megalithic monuments of Stonehenge.

name: Palace of Westminster and Westminster Abbey
description: Gothic Abbey, medieval palace and St Margaret's Church on the Thames.

name: Tower of London
description: Fortress founded in 1066 with the White Tower and Crown Jewels.

name: Canterbury Cathedral
description: Mother Church of the worldwide Anglican communion with medieval architecture.

name: Edinburgh Old and New Towns
description: Scottish capital with medieval Old Town dominated by castle and 18th-century New Town.

name: Blenheim Palace
description: English Baroque palace built for John Churchill, birthplace of Winston Churchill.

name: Palace of Versailles
description: Royal residence built for Louis XIV with its famous Hall of Mirrors and gardens.

name: Mont Saint Michel and its Bay
description: Island monastery rising from tidal flats off the Normandy coast.

name: Chartres Cathedral
description: High Gothic cathedral with intact medieval stained glass and sculpted portals.

name: Prehistoric Sites and Decorated Caves of the Vezere Valley

name: Pont du Gard

name: Paris Banks of the Seine

name: Carcassonne Historic Fortified City

name: Alhambra Generalife and Albayzin Granada

name: Burgos Cathedral

name: Works of Antoni Gaudi

name: Altamira Cave and Palaeolithic Cave Art of Northern Spain

name: Seville Cathedral Alcazar and Archivo de Indias

name: Old Town of Salamanca

name: Route of Santiago de Compostela

name: Cologne Cathedral

name: Aachen Cathedral

name: Bauhaus and its Sites in Weimar Dessau and Bernau
description: Buildings created by the Bauhaus school between 1919 and 1933.

name: Swiss Alps Jungfrau-Aletsch

description: Baroque palaces, imperial buildings and fin-de-siècle architecture of the Habsburgs.

name: Historic Centre of Prague

name: Historic Centre of Warsaw

name: Kraków Historic Centre

name: Auschwitz Birkenau

description: Symbol of Russian power with fortifications, palaces, churches and State Historical Museum.

name: Historic Centre of Saint Petersburg
description: City founded by Peter the Great with imperial palaces including the Hermitage Museum.

name: Old Town of Riga

name: Budapest including the Banks of the Danube

name: Plitvice Lakes National Park

name: Historic Areas of Istanbul
description: Byzantine and Ottoman capital with Hagia Sophia, Topkapi Palace and Blue Mosque.

name: Göreme National Park and Rock Sites of Cappadocia
description: Fairy chimneys and rock-cut dwellings and churches of Cappadocia.

name: Hierapolis-Pamukkale

name: Ephesus

name: Troy

name: Hattusha the Hittite Capital

name: Independence Hall

name: Statue of Liberty

name: Yellowstone National Park

name: Grand Canyon National Park
description: Spectacular canyon carved by the Colorado River with geological record of 2 billion years.

name: Yosemite National Park

description: Active volcanoes Kilauea and Mauna Loa in the Hawaiian island chain.

name: Canadian Rocky Mountain Parks
description: Banff, Jasper, Kootenay and Yoho parks with mountain peaks, glaciers and fossil sites.

name: Old Quebec

name: City of Cuzco

name: Chan Chan Archaeological Zone

name: Historic Town of Ouro Preto

name: Iguaçu National Park
description: Spectacular Iguaçu Falls with subtropical rainforest on the Brazil-Argentina border.

name: Iguazu National Park

name: Cartagena Port City Fortifications

name: City of Quito

name: Pyramids of Giza and Sphinx
description: Ancient pyramids of Khufu, Khafre and Menkaure and the Great Sphinx of Giza.

name: Ksar of Ait-Ben-Haddou

name: Kairouan

name: Carthage

name: Petra

name: Wadi Rum

name: Ancient City of Damascus
description: One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world.

name: Ancient City of Aleppo
description: City at the crossroads of trade routes between Asia and the Mediterranean.

name: Ruins of Palmyra

name: Persepolis

name: Naqsh-e Jahan Square Isfahan

name: Pasargadae

name: Historic City of Yazd

name: Qadisha Valley and Forest of Cedars of God

name: Serengeti National Park

name: Ngorongoro Conservation Area
description: Volcanic caldera with extraordinarily dense animal populations.

name: Kilimanjaro National Park

name: Ruins of Kilwa Kisiwani and Songo Mnara

name: Okavango Delta

name: Victoria Falls Mosi-oa-Tunya

name: Island of Gorée

name: Aksum

name: Lalibela Rock-Hewn Churches

name: Fasil Ghebbi Gondar Region

name: Simien National Park

name: Lamu Old Town

name: Lake Turkana National Parks

name: Mount Kenya National Park and Forest

name: Robben Island
